app.headline: Plan, compare, and pursue benefits with official government evidence.
app.lede: Screen benefits, compare what-if scenarios, browse the official catalog, and leave with a clearer action plan grounded in government sources.
home.trustPrivate: Your data is private and never stored.
home.trustSources: Data sourced directly from official government programs.
home.categoriesHint: Select at least one category to find benefits you may qualify for and information on how to apply.
home.questionEmpty: Start a session to begin the screener.
home.noMoreQuestions: No more questions queued. Results are ready.
home.noProgramsFound: We couldn't find any benefit programs matching your selection. Try broadening your categories or selecting a different state.
home.selectCategory: Select at least one category before applying your selections.
home.chooseScope: Choose a screening scope before starting your session.
home.chooseStateMsg: Please choose a state before starting state or combined screening.
home.creatingSessionState: Creating session and loading state benefits...
home.answerRequired: Please choose an answer before continuing.
home.syncSummary: Official sources refreshed. Crawled {crawled} program sites and added {added} direct government page sources.
home.adminPlaceholder: Only needed when admin protection is enabled
home.adminHelp: Used only for review queue and source refresh actions. Stored in this browser tab until you clear it.
home.adminSaved: Admin key saved for this browser tab.
home.lifeIntakeLabel: Tell us about the struggle in your own words
home.lifeIntakePlaceholder: Example: I lost my job, I’m behind on rent, and I’m trying to figure out whether there is help for food and medical coverage.
home.lifeIntakeHint: You do not need to classify the problem first. We will turn your description into suggested categories, likely facts, and the next questions that matter most.
home.lifeApply: Use these suggestions in the screener
home.lifeChatHint: Tell me what is going on, and I will ask one short follow-up at a time.
home.lifeChatEmpty: Start by describing what you are dealing with, and I will translate it into benefit areas and next questions.
home.lifeChatWelcome: Hi. I’m Zobo. Tell me a little about what’s going on, and I’ll help you sort through the next steps.
home.lifeChatPlaceholder: Try: I lost my job, I am behind on rent, and I need help with food.
home.lifeChatPlaceholderYesNo: A short yes or no is enough here.
home.lifeChatPlaceholderState: Reply with a state like CA, California, or New York.
home.lifeChatNavigate: You may also want to go to
home.lifeChatStatusEmpty: Tell me what is going on to get started.
home.lifeChatStatusWaitingDetails: Tell me a little more so I can point you in the right direction.
home.lifeChatStatusReady: I have one quick follow-up ready.
home.lifeChatStatusDone: I have enough to move forward.
home.lifeNoMissing: No critical follow-up questions are missing right now.
home.lifeApplyDone: Assistant suggestions copied into the screener controls.
home.lifeStarted: Started screening from the assistant's suggestions.
home.lifeDescriptionRequired: Tell me what is going on so I can help.
results.title: Results
results.noSession: No active session. Start a screening first.
results.noFederal: No federal results yet. Answer more questions to improve matches.
results.noState: No state results yet. Answer more questions to improve matches.
results.federalOnlyFinished: This is a federal-only session, so no state results are shown.
results.federalOnlyHidden: State results are hidden while Federal only scope is selected.
dashboard.title: Planning Dashboard
dashboard.empty: No active session yet. Start a session to unlock planning summary, action steps, and missing-fact analysis.
dashboard.goHome: Go to Home to start a session
dashboard.noDocuments: No documents required yet. Complete screening to see what you need.
dashboard.noPlanningNotes: Planning notes will appear after your session has results.
dashboard.noSessionLink: No active session. Start a screening first.
whatif.title: What-If Lab
whatif.noSession: No active session. Start a screening first.
whatif.description: Run scenario comparisons without changing your saved answers. These presets show how the result set changes if a life fact shifts.
whatif.startThenRun: Start a session, then run a scenario.
whatif.noNewMatches: No new positive matches in this scenario.
whatif.noImprovements: No status improvements in this scenario.
whatif.startFirst: Start a session before running scenarios.
explorer.title: Program Explorer
explorer.description: Hybrid Explorer combines plain-English need descriptions with the official catalog. It may use Gemini for interpretation when configured, and always falls back to grounded local matching.
explorer.describePlaceholder: Example: I lost a family member in California and need help with funeral costs and survivor benefits.
explorer.empty: Use the explorer to browse the current official program catalog.
explorer.noMatch: No programs matched this search. Try a broader description or remove a filter.
explorer.minChars: Enter at least 2 characters for exact keyword search.
explorer.localizedSummary: Showing how your need was interpreted from the local grounded catalog.
card.noMissingFacts: No missing facts for this current pass.
card.noDocuments: No document checklist available for this program.
card.dataGathered: Data gathered from official government websites
card.noApplicationPath: No official application path is available for this result yet.
breadth.focused: Focused keeps the screen short and concentrates on the strongest program signals.
breadth.balanced: Balanced explores a moderate number of possible programs before stopping.
breadth.broad: Broad keeps exploring longer so the screener can cover more possible programs.
depth.light: High-level keeps questions broad, usually yes/no or simple category prompts.
depth.standard: Standard mixes broad screeners with some more specific follow-ups.
depth.detailed: Detailed asks for exact amounts, dates, durations, and other precise facts when available.
scenario.limitedIncome: If I had limited income and resources
scenario.limitedIncomeDesc: Useful for cash-assistance or SSI-style planning.
scenario.disability: If I had a qualifying disability
scenario.disabilityDesc: Tests disability-related pathways and work-limitation rules.
scenario.military: If I had active-duty military service
scenario.militaryDesc: Checks whether service history opens veteran benefits.
scenario.familyDeath: If I had a recent family death event
scenario.familyDeathDesc: Explores survivor and funeral assistance pathways.
intake.fact.applicant_ability_to_work: Unable to work for a year or more
intake.fact.applicant_paid_into_SS: Worked and paid Social Security taxes
intake.fact.recent_job_loss: Recent job loss or reduced hours
intake.fact.current_student: Currently in school or job training
intake.fact.needs_health_coverage: Needs health coverage or medical help
server.step.startState: Start on the official state social services website.
server.step.useStateTools: Use the state's official benefit tools or program pages to choose the benefit you need.
server.step.followStateInstructions: Follow the state's official instructions for documents, local office contact, or online submission.
server.step.reviewEligibility: Review the official government eligibility source used for this match.
server.step.openOfficial: Open the official government page to start or continue the application.
server.step.useSamePage: Use the same official page for required documents, status checks, or agency contact details.
server.note.strongMatches: You already have {count} strong match{plural} to pursue on official government sites.
server.note.possibleMatches: You have {count} possible match{plural}; answering a few more questions should tighten these.
server.note.biggestGap: The biggest information gap right now is: {label}.
server.note.chooseState: Choose a state to unlock official state benefit pathways.
admin.invalidKey: The saved admin key was rejected. Update it and try again.
admin.requiredKey: Admin key required. Enter it in the Admin access field to use review and sync actions.
dynamic.question.dob: What is your date of birth?
dynamic.question.paidIntoSS: Did you ever work and pay U.S. Social Security taxes?
dynamic.question.limitedIncome: Do you have limited income and resources?
dynamic.hint.limitedIncome: This is a broad screening question, not a final determination.
dynamic.question.disability: Do you have a disability or qualifying illness?
dynamic.question.unableToWork: Are you unable to work for a year or more because of your disability?
dynamic.question.activeMilitary: Did you serve in the active military, naval, or air service?
dynamic.question.serviceDisabilityDetail: Was your disability caused or made worse by your active-duty military service?
dynamic.question.familyDeathDetailed: Did you recently experience the death of an immediate family member?
dynamic.question.dateOfDeath: What was the date of death?
dynamic.hint.dateOfDeath: If you do not know the exact date, enter an approximate one.
dynamic.question.covidDeath: Was the person's death COVID-19 related?
dynamic.question.deathInUs: Did the person die in the U.S.?
dynamic.hint.deathInUs: Including Puerto Rico and U.S. territories.
dynamic.question.householdIncome: What is your approximate annual household income?
dynamic.hint.householdIncome: For reference, the 2024 federal poverty level is $15,060/year for a single-person household. Many programs use 130–200% of this threshold.
dynamic.question.disabilityType: What type of disability or condition do you have?
dynamic.hint.disabilityType: Select all that apply. Under SSA rules, a qualifying disability must significantly limit your ability to perform basic work activities.
dynamic.question.monthsUnableToWork: How many months has your condition prevented you from working?
dynamic.hint.monthsUnableToWork: SSDI requires inability to engage in substantial gainful activity for at least 12 consecutive months (42 U.S.C. § 423(d)(1)(A)).
dynamic.hint.approxAge: A rough age is enough for a quick check.
dynamic.question.exactDob: What is your exact date of birth?
dynamic.hint.exactDob: Full retirement age varies: 66 for those born 1943–1954, increasing to 67 for those born 1960 or later (42 U.S.C. § 416(l)).
dynamic.hint.activeMilitary: VA benefits require active duty service. Reserve/National Guard service may qualify if activated under federal orders (38 U.S.C. § 101(2)).
dynamic.question.serviceDisabilitySimple: Was your disability related to military service?
dynamic.hint.serviceDisabilityDetailed: VA disability compensation requires a service-connected condition with a disability rating of at least 10% (38 U.S.C. § 1110).
dynamic.question.familyDeathSimple: Did you recently lose a family member?
dynamic.hint.familyDeathDetailed: Survivor benefits eligibility depends on your relationship to the deceased and their work history (42 U.S.C. § 402).
dynamic.question.stateCode: Which state or territory do you want state benefits for?
dynamic.hint.stateCode: State benefits vary by state and are kept separate from federal matches.
dynamic.summary.retirement: Monthly retirement income for people who worked and paid Social Security taxes.
dynamic.summary.ssdi: Monthly disability benefits for people who paid into Social Security and cannot work due to disability.
dynamic.summary.ssi: Monthly support for older adults and people with disabilities who have limited income and resources.
dynamic.summary.vaDisability: Tax-free monthly payments for veterans whose disability was caused or worsened by active-duty service.
dynamic.summary.survivor: Monthly Social Security payments for eligible family members after a worker dies.
dynamic.summary.funeral: Financial help for funeral or burial costs for someone who died of COVID-19 in the U.S.
dynamic.reason.paidIntoSS: You worked and paid Social Security taxes.
dynamic.reason.retirementAge: You are at least retirement age.
dynamic.reason.limitedIncome: You have limited income and resources.
dynamic.reason.disability: You have a disability or qualifying illness.
dynamic.reason.unableToWork: You cannot work for a year or more because of your disability.
dynamic.reason.activeMilitary: You served in the active military.
dynamic.reason.serviceDisability: Your disability was caused or worsened by active-duty service.
dynamic.reason.familyDeath: You recently experienced the death of a family member.
dynamic.reason.covidDeath: The deceased's death was COVID-19 related.
dynamic.reason.diedInUs: The deceased died in the U.S.
dynamic.amount.ssi: Up to $943/month for individuals, $1,415/month for couples (2024 rates).
dynamic.amount.retirement: Monthly amount depends on work history and claiming age.
dynamic.amount.ssdi: Monthly amount depends on work history.
dynamic.amount.vaDisability: Amount depends on disability rating and dependents.
dynamic.amount.survivor: Monthly amount depends on the worker's record and your relationship.
dynamic.amount.funeral: Reimbursement amount depends on eligible funeral expenses.
dynamic.document.w2: W-2 forms or self-employment tax returns
dynamic.document.w2CurrentPriorDesc: Earnings records for the current and prior year
dynamic.document.medicalRecordsDesc: Documentation of your disability from doctors, hospitals, or clinics
dynamic.document.incomeResourcesDesc: Pay stubs, bank statements, or benefit award letters
dynamic.document.livingArrangementDesc: Lease, mortgage statement, or letter from landlord
dynamic.document.citizenship: Proof of citizenship or immigration status
dynamic.document.citizenshipDesc: Birth certificate, passport, or immigration documents
dynamic.document.dd214Desc: Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty
dynamic.document.serviceEvidenceDesc: Evidence linking your disability to military service
dynamic.document.deathCertificateDesc: Certified copy of the death certificate
dynamic.document.relationshipProofDesc: Marriage certificate, birth certificate, or adoption papers
dynamic.document.covidDeathCertificateDe: Must attribute the death to COVID-19
dynamic.document.funeralReceiptsDesc: Itemized receipts or contracts from funeral providers
dynamic.document.usResidencyDesc: For the person who incurred the funeral expenses
dynamic.document.femaAppNumberDesc: If you previously applied for other FEMA assistance
